cabinet.title: The Firm
cabinet.description: Pacific Blue Consulting, an independent consulting firm founded in 2017 by Pascal Bazer-Bachi, former Director of Civil Aviation for French Polynesia.

hero.label: The Firm
hero.title: A firm built on field experience

manifeste.p1: In a territory where every island is a world of its own, where European regulation meets Pacific realities, where an aerodrome handling 200 passengers a week must meet the same requirements as an international hub - you need people who know how to build.
manifeste.p2: Pacific Blue Consulting was born of this conviction: island territories deserve the same rigour, the same ambition and the same quality of consulting as major metropolises, but with an intimate understanding of what makes them unique.
manifeste.p3: Since 2017, we have created airlines, certified airports, secured construction works on operating platforms, supported the ecological transition, trained managers, and structured food and craft supply chains. Because the sovereignty of a territory is built on all of these fronts at once.
manifeste.p4: Our method is nothing mysterious: understand before recommending, listen before structuring, and never deliver a document without making sure it can actually be implemented by those who will own it.
manifeste.p5: None of our projects ever starts from scratch. Like the spiral of the shell we use as our emblem, every mission builds on the ones before it - and with each turn, the perspective is wider, the experience richer, and the ability to make structural choices more assured.

portrait.name: Pascal Bazer-Bachi
portrait.imageAlt: Pascal Bazer-Bachi — Founder of Pacific Blue Consulting
portrait.bioP1: I work for island territories. Over the years, that has become my centre of gravity.
portrait.bioP2: I started in civil aviation on the ground in 1993. From there, I worked my way up every rung — operations, engineering, European satellite operations as Director of EGNOS Operations, territorial regulation in Picardy, safety and security oversight in French Polynesia, then heading the Civil Aviation Authority of French Polynesia and its network of forty-three airports spread across five archipelagos. And finally running a Polynesian airline, this time as an operator. Thirty years seeing the profession from every angle, which changes the way you then support the organisations that practise it.
portrait.bioP3: I founded Pacific Blue Consulting in 2019 to turn that experience into a useful instrument for the public and private actors of the Pacific. When we are entrusted with a project, we do not stop at recommendations: we commit, we own the trade-offs, we stay through to the end. The firm works on structural transformations — aviation, environment, infrastructure, territorial supply chains — as a multidisciplinary team, with partners chosen for their expertise and their standards.
portrait.bioP4Strong: My job is to leave organisations stronger after my work than before.
portrait.quote: "I see consulting as a lever for building capability. My goal is not for my clients to need me indefinitely - it is for them to be better equipped after my work than before. Curiously, that is often what brings them back."

ecosysteme.intro: Pacific Blue Consulting is your single point of contact and the guarantor of quality on every engagement. But some projects call for complementary skills: that is why we rely on a trusted ecosystem, built up over more than 60 missions.

timeline.label: Our journey
timeline.title: A history of expertise and commitment

2017.title: Founding of Pacific Blue Consulting
2017.description: Creation of the independent consulting firm in French Polynesia to support public and private stakeholders in aviation and airport services.

2017-2020.title: First structural missions
2017-2020.description: Support to Tahiti Nui Helicopters (2017-2018). Certification of Islands Airline and start of the modernisation of Air Loyauté (2019). Support for the Vinci Airports bid and contribution to the creation of Air Bora Bora (2020).

2021-2023.title: Multi-territory deployment
2021-2023.description: Compliance and certification for Air Loyauté (Air Operator Certificate, Part-145, Part-CAMO). Inter-island Marquesas route with Tahiti Air Charter. CNAM training programmes and creation of a Part-147 centre with Air Formation. DIREN biodiversity certification.

2024-2025.title: Ecological transition and innovation
2024-2025.description: Master plans for Moorea and Huahine airports. Carbon footprint of the DAC-PF and decarbonisation roadmap. 10 Aerodrome Safety Impact Studies delivered. Innovative marine airports with Terciel. TAVIVAT project (food sovereignty).

2025.title: South Pacific reach
2025.description: Feasibility study for a regional air route Tahiti - Cook Islands - Tonga - Samoa - Fiji. More than 60 missions delivered since the firm's creation.

coquillage.label: Our identity
coquillage.title: The shell, emblem of Pacific Blue
coquillage.p1: The visual identity of Pacific Blue Consulting is born from a stylised shell whose spiral opens and grows as it unfolds. This spiral suggests that no project ever starts from scratch: we revisit familiar themes — safety, service continuity, business models, governance — but with each turn, the perspective is wider, the experience richer, and the ability to make structural choices more assured.
coquillage.p2Before: The shell is also a tribute to Polynesia and the great Pacific Ocean, where it is at once a daily object, a symbol of the lagoons, and a tangible trace of the bond between land and sea. It echoes the
coquillage.p2After: , the conch you blow to signal your presence, announce an event or welcome navigators on their approach — like a clear and benevolent call across the vastness.
coquillage.p3: This image expresses the vocation of PBC: welcoming, guiding and securing our clients' trajectories in a complex environment, with deep respect for the territories and the people we serve.

support.p1: Pascal Bazer-Bachi sees consulting as a lever for building team capability: as an accredited trainer and designer-facilitator of programmes in project management, change leadership, managerial culture and entrepreneurial support, he works as much on content as on day-to-day working practices.
support.p2: An accredited professional and executive coach, he supports executives, managers and project leaders in clarifying their challenges, structuring their decisions and embodying transformations - whether in public policy, territorial projects, infrastructure or local economic initiatives.

mobilites.title: Mobility & Air transport
mobilites.subtitle: Building, structuring and securing aviation operations
mobilites.approach:
Air transport in an island setting is not just another service - it is the vital link between communities separated by the ocean. Every airline, every route, every aerodrome is a link in a chain of territorial continuity on which the health, economy and social cohesion of entire communities depend.

Pacific Blue Consulting works across the full lifecycle of an air operator: from the initial idea to daily operations, from the first page of the business plan to maintaining certifications year after year. Our strength is to speak the language of regulators (DGAC, EASA), of investors and of flight crews all at once.

We have built airlines where none existed, brought struggling operators back into compliance, designed routes through mountainous terrain, and supported concession bids against the world's largest groups.
coverage: Airline creation and structuring (from legal status to Air Operator Certificate)
Business plans, economic models and commercial strategy
Operating certification and operational documentation (MANEX, safety and security management manuals)
EASA compliance: Part-145, Part-M, Part-CAMO, Part-OPS
Regulatory audits and compliance action plans
Aerodrome Safety Impact Studies (EISA) - 10 delivered since 2024
Airport security expertise (audits, governance, TSA interface)
Inter-island route strategy and territorial continuity
Drones and UAVs: strategy, operations manuals, aerial imagery
Aerodrome rescue and fire-fighting (SSLIA), AFIS and maintenance documentation
mobilites.focusText:
Pascal Bazer-Bachi is one of the few experts in the Pacific to combine senior leadership within the regulatory authority and hands-on consulting practice in airport safety and security.

Since 2024, 10 Aerodrome Safety Impact Studies delivered on platforms ranging from the Tuamotu atolls to Tahiti-Faa'a international airport (wide-body B777/A350, work on operating sites, multi-stakeholder coordination). Methodology aligned with national and European requirements; residual risks placed within the ALARP zone.

On security: experience chairing Airport Security Operational Committees (COS) and Airport Security Commissions, and as the interface with the US TSA on the Tahiti-Faa'a platform.
missions: Creation of Tahiti Nui Helicopters (2017-2018)
Certification of Islands Airline - 47 documents (2019)
Creation of Air Bora Bora (2020-2023)
Modernisation of Air Loyauté (2019-2023)
Support to Vinci Airports - Faa'a concession bid (2020)
10 Aerodrome Safety Impact Studies for DAC-Pf and Etik Pf/AdT (2024-2025)
Marquesas safety study - 75 events, 4 mountainous fields (2021-2022)
Wallis-Futuna Public Service safety study - 100+ events (2023-2024)
South Pacific regional air route (2025)
SSLIA regulations and AFIS manuals for the DAC-Pf (2024-2025)
mobilites.result: Aviation operations launched on solid foundations, certifications obtained and maintained, risks under control - because territorial continuity is not a concept, it is the daily reality of island life.

infrastructures.title: Infrastructure & Territories
infrastructures.subtitle: Designing the platforms that connect island communities
infrastructures.approach:
A coral aerodrome with an 800-metre runway in the Tuamotu. A port in the Loyalty Islands that must absorb vital freight traffic. A private runway on a protected atoll. Every island infrastructure is a special case - and yet each must comply with international standards designed for very different contexts.

Pacific Blue Consulting knows how to navigate this equation. We design master plans that account at once for regulatory constraints, land realities, the financial capacity of local authorities and the expectations of the communities they serve.
missions: Province of the Loyalty Islands (DPA) - ports and airports (2024-2029)
Master plan for Moorea and Huahine airports (2024-2025)
Closure of non-conformities on the Tetiaroa runway (2024-2025)
Polynesian aerodrome network - MLA (2021)
Feasibility studies in Lifou (Wanaham and Hmeleck - 2022)
Terciel - floating marine airports (2022-2030)
Temae airport road study - Municipality of Moorea

environnement.title: Environment & Sovereignty
environnement.subtitle: Supporting the ecological transition and resilience of territories
environnement.approach:
The sovereignty of an island territory plays out on several fronts at once: reducing its dependence on fossil fuels, protecting biodiversity that is unique in the world, feeding its population with its own resources, and showcasing the know-how that shapes its identity.

Pacific Blue Consulting supports this transition with the same rigour we bring to civil aviation: fact-based diagnostic, quantified targets, realistic roadmap, and long-term support. We do not sell environmental rhetoric - we build mechanisms that work.
missions: Carbon footprint of the DAC-Pf and decarbonisation roadmap (2025)
DIREN biodiversity certification (2021-2023)
Update of the environmental impact assessment framework (2025-2026)
Market study of local staple foods - TAVIVAT project (2025-2026)
Steering of the TAVIVAT project - DAG (2026-2027)
Pandanus drying facility - Traditional Crafts Service (2025-2026)

transformation.title: Transformation, Skills & Business creation
transformation.subtitle: Supporting those who transform, those who learn, and those who start a business
transformation.approach:
The most ambitious projects fail when the organisations that must deliver them are not ready — and the most dynamic territories stall when the talent that could transform them does not dare to take the leap into entrepreneurship. That is why a core part of our work is to strengthen teams, clarify responsibilities, equip managers, train those who will make tomorrow's decisions, and support entrepreneurs through the creation of their business.

Whether we are supporting an operator in the middle of a transformation, a team that needs training, or an entrepreneur creating their first business, our conviction is the same: it is the women and the men who bring organisations to life. And it is by equipping them, clarifying what is expected of them and securing their choices that we build sustainable trajectories.
coverage: Training and change management (project management, leadership, compliance)
Professional and executive coaching for leaders and managers
Design and facilitation of training programmes
Creation and structuring of accredited training centres (Part-147, etc.)
HR structuring, organisation and clarification of responsibilities
Support for new business creation: situation analysis, choice of legal status, registration paperwork
Business plan development and preparation of funding applications (banks, SOFIDEP, ADIE, public schemes)
Follow-up of initial tax and social obligations for newly created businesses
transformation.focusText:
Starting a business in French Polynesia means crossing an administrative threshold that often feels insurmountable: CDFE, RCS, DICP, CPS, CAPL, banking, insurance. Pacific Blue Consulting supports future Polynesian entrepreneurs along the entire journey, from choosing the right legal status to effective registration, from business plan to securing funding.

Our service does not replace public schemes — CCISM, ADIE, SOFIDEP, DGAE — it helps you make full use of them. We take on the heaviest part of the formalities: situation analysis, choice of path (sole trader licence, company, agricultural or otherwise), preparation and submission of applications, and follow-up through to obtaining the necessary registration numbers.

Our offering:

• Sole-trader licence (patente) — to operate as an individual.

• Company creation — limited liability or simplified joint-stock companies (SARL, EURL, SAS) for projects requiring a corporate structure.

• Agricultural project creation — farming, livestock, lagoon fisheries, pearl farming.

• Business plan and funding support — bank applications, SOFIDEP, ADIE and public schemes.

• End-to-end creation with funding — integrated project combining the creation of the structure and the mobilisation of financing.

• Start-up follow-up — securing the first months of activity and the initial tax and social deadlines.
missions: CNAM Managers' Club (2021-2022)
Air Formation Part-147 centre - turnkey creation (2022)
Organisation & HR for Air Loyauté (2020-2023)
Project management training - HSF (2024-2025)
Support for the creation of sole-trader and corporate businesses
Preparation of funding applications with banks, SOFIDEP and ADIE
Start-up support for new Polynesian businesses
transformation.result: More capable teams, clearer organisations, better-equipped managers, project owners who move from idea to a registered business, and professional or entrepreneurial trajectories made secure. Because a territory's best investment is its talent and those who dare to build.
